head: 8e9be379
fix: permissions.web.fetch: allow + sandbox.backend: noop in worker reyn.local.yaml

scenarios.reply_excerpt: Python 3.12 introduces several key new features, including: PEP 695: Type Parameter Syntax... PEP 701: Syntactic formalization of f-strings...
scenarios.notes: web__fetch invoked successfully (status 200, content_length 50000, total_length 103774). No permission_denied event. Reply names PEP 695 + PEP 701 + acknowledges other improvements. permissions.web.fetch: allow injection effective.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: The command failed because the Python interpreter could not be found due to a file system sandbox restriction.
scenarios.notes: exec__sandboxed_exec invoked with backend=seatbelt (NOT noop as configured). sandboxed_exec_completed returncode=-6, stderr shows 'file system sandbox blocked open()' for /Users/yasudatetsuya/.pyenv/versions/3.12.7/lib/libpython3.12.dylib. Direct config load via load_config() returns backend='noop' correctly, but runtime path emitted backend='seatbelt' event — fix did NOT propagate to the running RouterCallerState/sandbox handler chain. Same OS env bug class as original B52; fix incomplete.
